perturbation.original_text: 2. Proprietary Information shall be held in stri ct confidence by Receiving Party and shall not be used by Receiving Party except for (i) the pur poses described in this Agreement, unless disclosure or further use is authorized or consented to in writing by Disclosing Party which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld or dela yed and/or (ii) the case where disclosure of the same is required under applicable law or by a gove rnmental order, rule or regulation or by the regulations of any relevant stock exchange or other governmental authority (provided that the Receiving party shall give written notice of such re quired disclosure to the other party prior to the disclosure). Such consent to disclose Proprietary Information to employees of Receiving Party or its affiliated company (“affiliated Receiving Party”) with a legitimate “need to know” and only for the purposes described in this Agreement is herewith given, but further consent shall be required for disclosure to others or authorization of use by others. For purposes of this Confidentiality Agreement, an "affiliated Receiving Party" mean s and includes a parent, if any, of Receiving Party and all present and future companies in whic h Receiving Party, or its parent, individually or collectively, directly or indirectly, through one or more intermediaries, owns or controls fifty percent (50%) or more of the outstanding stock having the right to vote for or appoint directors thereof. Prior to any use or disclosure of Propr ietary Information by or to another, Receiving Party shall ensure that the re cipient has entered into an agreement limiting the recipient's disclosure and use of Proprietary Informa tion consistent with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Proprietary Information shall be held in stri ct confidence by Receiving Party and shall not be used by Receiving Party except for (i) the pur poses described in this Agreement and/or (ii) the case where disclosure of the same is required under applicable law or by a gove rnmental order, rule or regulation or by the regulations of any relevant stock exchange or other governmental authority (provided that the Receiving party shall give written notice of such re quired disclosure to the other party prior to the disclosure). Prior to any use or disclosure of Propr ietary Information by or to another, Receiving Party shall ensure that the re cipient has entered into an agreement limiting the recipient's disclosure and use of Proprietary Informa tion consistent with this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause that states 'unless disclosure or further use is authorized or consented to in writing by Disclosing Party which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld or dela yed' and 'Such consent to disclose Proprietary Information to employees of Receiving Party or its affiliated company (“affiliated Receiving Party”) with a legitimate “need to know” and only for the purposes described in this Agreement is herewith given, but further consent shall be required for disclosure to others or authorization of use by others. For purposes of this Confidentiality Agreement, an "affiliated Receiving Party" mean s and includes a parent, if any, of Receiving Party and all present and future companies in whic h Receiving Party, or its parent, individually or collectively, directly or indirectly, through one or more intermediaries, owns or controls fifty percent (50%) or more of the outstanding stock having the right to vote for or appoint directors thereof.' from Section 2, we create an in-text contradiction with Section 5, which states 'No other existing Agreement between parties, if any, are modified or terminated by this Agreemen t.' Now, the Receiving Party cannot disclose the information with written consent by the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. No rights or obligations other than those expr essed and recited herein are to be implied from this Agreement. In particular, no licenses are hereby granted directly or indirectly under any patent, copyright, or trademark now held by or wh ich may be obtained by, or which is licensable by Disclosing Party, including but not limited to, any license to make, use or sell any product embodying any Proprietary Information. No othe r existing Agreement between parties, if any, are modified or terminated by this Agreemen t. No warranty or representation is made by Disclosing Party hereto that any information transmitted by it hereunde r is patentable or copyrightable, or that any such information involves concepts or embodiments that are free of infringement of other rights. Disclosing Party here to confers no right to Receiving Party to use in advertising, publicity, or otherwise any trademar k or trade name of Disclosing Party, nor confers any authorization to Receiving Party to act as an agent on its behalf for any purpose.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 2 allowed disclosure with the Disclosing Party's written consent and to affiliated parties with a 'need to know'. This is removed in the changed text. Section 5 states that no other existing agreement between parties are modified or terminated by this Agreement. Removing the clause about consent and affiliated parties from Section 2 creates an in-text contradiction because it alters the Receiving Party's ability to disclose information, which conflicts with Section 5's statement that existing agreements are not modified.

perturbation.original_text: 3. To enable the Disclosing Party to disclo se “technical data” to the Receiving Party in compliance with the requirements of the U.S. Commerce Department’s Export Administrative Regulations, the Receiving Party hereby gives its assurance to the Disclosing Party that the Receiving Party will not knowingly, unless prior written authorization is obtained from the Disclosing Party and the U.S. Commerce Departme nt, export, re-export or otherwise disclose, directly or indirectly, any “technical data” (or computer software and any related documentation) received from the Disclosing Party which is not otherwise available to the general public, nor allow the direct product thereof to be shipped directly or indirectly, to any of the countries published on the restrictive list promulgated a nd amended from time to time by the U.S. Department of Commerce. Notwithstanding any other provision of this Agre ement, this Paragraph 3 shall survive any termination or expirati on of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. The Receiving Party hereby gives its assurance to the Disclosing Party that the Receiving Party will not knowingly, export, re-export or otherwise disclose, directly or indirectly, any technical data received from the Disclosing Party which is not otherwise available to the general public, nor allow the direct product thereof to be shipped directly or indirectly, to any of the countries published on the restrictive list promulgated a nd amended from time to time by the U.S. Department of Commerce. Notwithstanding any other provision of this Agre ement, this Paragraph 3 shall survive any termination or expirati on of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'To enable the Disclosing Party to disclo se “technical data” to the Receiving Party in compliance with the requirements of the U.S. Commerce Department’s Export Administrative Regulations, unless prior written authorization is obtained from the Disclosing Party and the U.S. Commerce Departme nt', Section 3 now implies an absolute ban on exporting technical data, potentially creating a contradiction with Section 2, which allows disclosures required by law or governmental order. Is the receiving party authorized to disclose by a governmental order? The deletion undermines the original intention of disclosing the technical data.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Proprietary Information shall be held in stri ct confidence by Receiving Party and shall not be used by Receiving Party except for (i) the pur poses described in this Agreement and/or (ii) the case where disclosure of the same is required under applicable law or by a gove rnmental order, rule or regulation or by the regulations of any relevant stock exchange or other governmental authority (provided that the Receiving party shall give written notice of such re quired disclosure to the other party prior to the disclosure). Prior to any use or disclosure of Propr ietary Information by or to another, Receiving Party shall ensure that the re cipient has entered into an agreement limiting the recipient's disclosure and use of Proprietary Informa tion consistent with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 3 allows disclosure of technical data in compliance with U.S. Commerce Department regulations, requiring prior authorization for export. The changed text removes this compliance clause, implying an absolute ban on exporting technical data, regardless of U.S. Commerce Department regulations. This directly contradicts Section 2, which allows disclosures required by law or governmental order. The changed Section 3 now conflicts with the possibility of legally required disclosures under Section 2, creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Immediately upon a request by the Disclosing Pa rty at any time, the Receiving Party will turn over to the Disclosing Party all Proprietary Inform ation of the Disclosing Party and all documents or media containing any such Proprietary Inform ation and any and all copies or extracts thereof or upon request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall certify in writing that all materials containing Proprietary Information (incl uding all copies thereof) have been destroyed. The Receiving Party understands th at nothing herein (a) requires the disclosure of any Proprietary Information of the Disclosing Party, which shall be disclosed if at all sole ly at the option of the Disclosing Party, or (b) requires the Disclosing Pa rty to proceed with any proposed transaction or relationship in connection with which Propr ietary Information may be disclosed.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Immediately upon a request by the Disclosing Pa rty at any time, the Receiving Party will turn over to the Disclosing Party all Proprietary Inform ation of the Disclosing Party and all documents or media containing any such Proprietary Inform ation and any and all copies or extracts thereof or upon request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall certify in writing that all materials containing Proprietary Information (incl uding all copies thereof) have been destroyed.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the clause that states 'The Receiving Party understands th at nothing herein (a) requires the disclosure of any Proprietary Information of the Disclosing Party, which shall be disclosed if at all sole ly at the option of the Disclosing Party, or (b) requires the Disclosing Pa rty to proceed with any proposed transaction or relationship in connection with which Propr ietary Information may be disclosed.', Section 4 now creates an in-text contradiction because if the receiving party believes they need information disclosed for legal reasons, it is not clear who to clarify this situation with because of the modified text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Proprietary Information shall be held in stri ct confidence by Receiving Party and shall not be used by Receiving Party except for (i) the pur poses described in this Agreement and/or (ii) the case where disclosure of the same is required under applicable law or by a gove rnmental order, rule or regulation or by the regulations of any relevant stock exchange or other governmental authority (provided that the Receiving party shall give written notice of such re quired disclosure to the other party prior to the disclosure). Prior to any use or disclosure of Propr ietary Information by or to another, Receiving Party shall ensure that the re cipient has entered into an agreement limiting the recipient's disclosure and use of Proprietary Informa tion consistent with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text contained a clause explicitly stating that the disclosing party was not required to disclose any proprietary information. The changed text omits this clause. The contradicted text (Section 2) states that disclosure is required if compelled by law. Without the omitted clause, there is no clear statement protecting the Disclosing Party from disclosing information, creating a possible contradiction with the receiving party's obligation to disclose under law in Section 2. The absence of the omitted clause introduces ambiguity regarding the disclosing party's rights not to disclose. This is a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6. This Non-Disclosure Agreement shall remain in full force and effect from the effective date indicated above. Either party may terminate this Agreement by giving the other Party sixty (60) days’ written notice. However the confidentiality obligations and all powers, rights and duties provided herein shall extend for a period of five (5) years following the termination of this Agreement. Neither Party shall assign this Ag reement to any third party, without the prior written consent of the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. This Non-Disclosure Agreement shall remain in full force and effect from the effective date indicated above. Either party may terminate this Agreement by giving the other Party sixty (60) days’ written notice. However the confidentiality obligations and all powers, rights and duties provided herein shall extend for a period of five (5) years following the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'Neither Party shall assign this Ag reement to any third party, without the prior written consent of the other Party.', Section 6 now contradicts Section 8, which speaks to disputes arising 'in connection with this Agreement.' Without the clause preventing assignment, it is now unclear which party is involved in a dispute.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. This Agreement shall be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of Delaware, without regard to conflicts of law principles. Any dispute, controversy and/or difference which may arise between the Parties out of or in relation to or in connection with this Agreement, or the breach thereof, which cannot be settled by amicable mutual accord without undue delay, shall be filed in the courts of Delaware.
perturbation.justification: The original text prevented assignment of the agreement without consent. The changed text removes this restriction. The contradicted text discusses dispute resolution between 'the Parties.' Without the assignment restriction, the 'Parties' involved in a dispute could change without the other party's consent, creating uncertainty as to who is bound by the dispute resolution clause. This creates a clear in-text contradiction by removing clarity about the parties involved, making enforcement unclear.

perturbation.original_text: 9. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreemen t of the Parties hereto with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes and can cels all prior communications, understandings and agreements between the Parties hereto relating to the Proprietary Information, whether written or oral, expressed or implied. The Parties agree that this Agreement is severable and that in the event any provision of this Agreement is held to be ille gal, invalid or enforceab le, the legality, validity and enforceability of the remaining provisi ons will not be affected or impaired.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreemen t of the Parties hereto with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes and can cels all prior communications, understandings and agreements between the Parties hereto relating to the Proprietary Information, whether written or oral, expressed or implied.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'The Parties agree that this Agreement is severable and that in the event any provision of this Agreement is held to be ille gal, invalid or enforceab le, the legality, validity and enforceability of the remaining provisi ons will not be affected or impaired.', this now contradicts the purpose of 'Injunctive Relief' as stated in section 7, because the Injunctive relief can only work with all the agreement valid. Because no longer has 'severable' clause, now if anything is illegaly binded, the Injunctive Relief is affected.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. The Parties acknowledge and agree that due to the unique nature of the Disclosing Party’s Proprietary Information, money damages will not be a sufficient remedy for a breach of this Agreement. Because a violation of any of the provisions of this Agreement will likely cause irreparable loss and harm which cannot be reasonably or adequately compensated by damages in an action at law, the Disclosing Party will be entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief or other appropriate equitable re lief as remedies for any such breach. Such remedies will not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement but will be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The removal of the severability clause in Section 9 directly impacts the enforceability of the Injunctive Relief outlined in Section 7. Section 7 relies on the agreement's provisions being valid for injunctive relief to be effective. Without the severability clause, if any part of the agreement is deemed illegal or unenforceable, it could jeopardize the entire agreement, including the Injunctive Relief provision. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because Section 7 assumes the agreement's validity, while Section 9, after the omission, no longer guarantees it.
